page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.6xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A primary differentiator of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 70866 GB of local HDD storage, coupled with an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

The hardware configuration of this instance makes it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive storage capacity and sequential data access without the premium cost of solid-state drives. Because it utilizes traditional HDD storage rather than NVMe SSDs, it represents a specific tradeoff: high density and capacity at a lower qualitative cost, but with lower random IOPS compared to flash-based alternatives. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention.

This instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads that process large volumes of sequential data. Suitable use cases include big data analytics, distributed file systems, data warehousing, large-scale log processing, and cold storage archives.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM and a massive 70866 GB of local HDD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The hardware profile offers high resource density and cost efficiency for massive data storage, trading off the high random IOPS of SSDs for dense, high-capacity local HDD storage. This server is suited for big data processing, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and large-scale log analysis.

tagline: A high-capacity storage instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and massive local HDD storage for sequential data workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
